answer:
The surface current density \( K \) is given by \( K = n m \sin \theta \), where \( n = 10^{27} \, \text{atoms/m}^3 \), \( m = 10^{-23} \, \text{A m}^2 \), and \( \theta = 45^\circ \).

Calculating: \( K = 10^{27} \times 10^{-23} \times \sin 45^\circ = 10^4 \times \frac{\sqrt{2}}{2} \approx 7070 \, \text{A/m} \).

The correct answer is \(\boxed{C}\).
